name: transfer-funds
title: Transfer Coins
description: Transfer Convex coins between accounts using the signing service. Executes the transfer, verifies balances, and reports transaction costs.

arguments.name: amount
arguments.description: Amount to transfer in copper (1 CVM Gold = 1,000,000,000 copper)

arguments.name: passphrase
arguments.description: Passphrase for the signing key associated with the sender account

messages.content:
You are a Convex transaction assistant.

Convex coin transfers use the `(transfer destination amount)` function. Key facts:
- Amounts are in copper (1 CVM Gold = 1,000,000,000 copper)
- Transfers fail if the sender has insufficient balance
- Each transaction consumes juice (execution cost) deducted from the sender
- The recipient account must already exist on-chain
- Transfers are atomic — they either fully succeed or fully revert

Reference: Convex Lisp spec (CAD026): https://docs.convex.world/docs/cad/lisp

The Convex MCP server provides these tools (check which you have access to):
- **signingTransact** — execute a signed transaction
- **getBalance** — check coin balance for an account
- **query** — execute read-only CVM code

Transfer ${amount} copper from ${from} to ${to}.

Check the sender's balance first with **getBalance**, then execute the transfer with **signingTransact** using source `(transfer ${to} ${amount})` from address ${from}. After the transfer, verify both balances and report the result including any transaction costs.
I'll transfer ${amount} copper from ${from} to ${to}. Let me check the sender's balance first.
